version_notes: v1.0.3 (2026-05-01, certification pruning): removed BICSI and FOA Specialty (CFOS) from the certifications list. Reviewer feedback flagged that the prior list rewarded jobs that mention BICSI even though Per Scholas LVFT doesn't teach BICSI — scoring jobs higher because they require something we don't grant misrepresents which roles a graduate is actually qualified for. Curriculum-earned certs are OSHA-10, NFPA 70E, and FOA CFOT only; the certifications list now reflects exactly that set. Side effect: jobs whose descriptions mention BICSI but include curriculum-aligned signals will still qualify on those signals, just without the +5 BICSI bump. Spot-check after rescoring expected: handful of borderline LOW jobs may drop below the 30 threshold to ADJACENT; that's the intended correction. v1.0.2 (2026-04-28, Tier A specificity expansion): empirical observation from first three New York City LVFT fetches — 60-146 jobs returned per run, ZERO Tier A title hits. Diagnosis: the 24-phrase v1.0.1 Tier A list captures the curriculum-named target placements (fiber optic / low voltage / structured cabling / telecom installer) but misses the carrier-side and contractor-side title vocabulary that fiber techs are actually posted under in the wild. Additions: (1) splicer-noun variants (cable splicer, fiber optic splicer, fiber splice technician, splicing technician); (2) inside-plant titles to mirror the existing OSP set (inside plant technician/installer, outside plant installer); (3) FTTP installer variants (fttp technician/installer, ftth installer); (4) carrier infrastructure titles (central office technician, headend technician); (5) telecom lineworker variants (telecom lineman, communications lineman, fiber lineman); (6) DAS / in-building wireless (das technician, das installer); (7) cabling installer variants (network cabling installer, low voltage cabling technician/installer, cable installation technician, cabling specialist, fiber optic cable technician/installer). Tier A grows from 24 → 49 phrases. Industry_context expanded with 'fttp', 'inside plant', 'central office', 'headend', 'splicer' so the gate stays satisfied for the new title vocab. Same `score_if_no_industry_context: 0` gate keeps false-positive risk near zero — a non-telecom 'splicing technician' or 'cable installer' will still land at 0+skills. v1.0.1 (2026-04-28, post-first-fetch precision pass): expanded employer_watchlist with 9 employers observed in the v1.0.0 first-fetch HIGH set that weren't on the seed list. Categories updated: fiber_isp_carrier += hotwire communications, ezee fiber (+2); low_voltage_contractors += teksystems, essintial, decisionpoint, mastec (+4); cabling_specialists += afl global, afl telecommunications, ascension property services (+3). Watchlist now totals 64 employers across 5 categories (was 55). Spot-checked 15 v1.0.0 HIGH jobs and ZERO false positives — taxonomy gates are well-calibrated; this pass is purely about giving the watchlist boost to the contractors actually doing the placements. v1.0.0 (2026-04-28): initial taxonomy derived from UCI 1019 (12 weeks, 330 instructional hours). Per Scholas-Imran scoping: title tiers folded from 4 to 2 (Tier C/D dropped); AV/Security Installer titles live in Tier B; 'IT Infrastructure Technician' placed in Tier B (curriculum-listed but generic enough to risk pulling IT-side roles; the industry-context gate handles the false positives); 'Network Engineer' / 'Network Architect' excluded as IT-engineer-level; 'Network Technician' allowed (Tier B). Score-if-no-industry-context applied to BOTH Tier A and Tier B — every LVFT match requires at least one fiber / cabling / low-voltage / telecom signal phrase in the description, else the title score collapses to 0 (e.g., a generic 'field technician' role at a non-telecom employer with no cabling vocabulary lands at 0+skills, well into ADJACENT). Geography: 100mi default radius per Imran (matches CFT after this release). Pilot: active at all 25 Per Scholas campuses from day one (no per-campus rollout phase like CFT had).

A.description: Direct synonyms — auto-pass to skill verification, highest title score (40 pts). Includes the five curriculum-listed target placements (Fiber Optic Technician, Low Voltage Technician, Telecom Installer, Network Cabling Technician, IT Infrastructure Technician) plus their common variants. v1.0.0: requires at least one fiber/cabling/telecom industry-context phrase in description; without one the title score collapses to 0 (`score_if_no_industry_context`). Catches the false-positive pattern where a generic 'field technician' or 'IT infrastructure technician' title at a non-telecom employer has no cabling content.

B.description: Family match (25 pts). Broader telecom / installation / AV / security titles that LVFT graduates can credibly land. v1.0.0: same `score_if_no_industry_context: 0` gate as Tier A — requires fiber / cabling / low-voltage / telecom signal in description. Also the v1.1.0 (CFT-style) `score_if_unverified` two-stage demotion: even with industry context, requires ≥3 core skill matches to keep full 25; otherwise drops to 10. AV/Security Installer titles folded in here per scoping decision (originally a Tier C in the design draft); Per Scholas's 4-day Low Voltage Systems module covers CCTV / IP cameras / access control directly.

C.description: Unused in v1.0.0 — kept in the schema so engine code that iterates A/B/C/D doesn't need conditional handling. Future LVFT taxonomy passes may use this slot for an OSP/aerial-lineman track or a wireless/DAS track if curriculum expands.

D.description: Unused in v1.0.0. Future LVFT passes may use this slot for a healthcare-IT-cabling track if Per Scholas signs healthcare partnerships that justify it.

title_exclusions.description: Hard reject if title contains any of these substrings.

description_disqualifiers.description: Hard reject if description_text contains any of these phrases.

experience.description: Regex-based experience filter (same shape as CFT). Reject when a captured year-count > max_years_allowed AND a 'required'-class word appears within ±50 chars of the match.

core_skills.description: Curriculum-confirmed skills taught extensively in UCI 1019 (≥4 days each, or foundational across multiple modules). Each match = 8 pts, capped at 40. Cert-bearing skills (OSHA 10, NFPA 70E, CFOT) appear here AND in `certifications` — known double-count, intentional for v1.0 to give certs full weight; will be reconciled if it produces inflated scores in real fetches.

specialized_skills.description: Hands-on lab skills with named systems and tools. Each match = 4 pts, capped at 20.

bonus_skills.description: Supporting topics. Each match = 1 pt, capped at 5. Only counts if at least one core skill OR industry context also matched (prevents random-job false positives).

industry_context.description: Fiber / cabling / low-voltage / telecom industry signals. Each match = 3 pts, capped at 10. Critically used as the gate for Tier A and Tier B `score_if_no_industry_context: 0` — every LVFT title match must produce at least one industry-context hit, else the title score collapses to 0.

healthcare_context.description: Unused in v1.0.0 (no Tier D in LVFT). Kept in schema for engine compatibility.

certifications.description: Curriculum-earned certifications ONLY — every cert in this list is granted to Per Scholas LVFT graduates. Industry certs that are commonly requested but not taught (BICSI, CFOS, etc.) were removed in v1.0.3 because rewarding jobs for requiring credentials we don't grant misrepresents which roles a graduate is actually qualified for. Each match = 5 pts, capped at 15.

employer_watchlist.description: Known target employers across the LVFT placement landscape. Match = +5 pts. v1.0.0 categories cover ISPs/carriers, hyperscale/colo (fiber inside DCs), low-voltage contractors, and cabling specialists. No `is_healthcare` flag set on any v1.0 category — Tier D is unused in LVFT so the healthcare gate doesn't apply.

experience_filter.description: API-side filter + post-fetch tightening. Same as CFT — Per Scholas graduates target 0–2 / 2–5 brackets.

post_fetch_penalty.description: If ai_experience_level is 2-5 BUT description requires >3 years, deduct 10 points.

geography.description: Per-campus geofencing using haversine distance. v1.0.0: 100mi radius per Imran (matches CFT 100mi after the parallel CFT update). LVFT roles often involve travel for installs so a wider commute zone is realistic.

formula: + bonus_skills_score (count * 1, capped at 5; requires core_match >= 1 OR industry_match >= 1)
Hard rejects (return REJECT regardless of score):

FIBER_TRACK.description: Strong fiber-side signal regardless of title — heavy fusion-splicing / OTDR / single-mode / multimode content suggests the job is genuinely fiber-focused vs. a generic cabling role.

COPPER_TRACK.description: Strong structured-cabling / copper signal — Cat5e/6, patch panels, TIA-568, punch-down content. Useful for distinguishing pure-copper installer roles from fiber-heavy ones.

AV_SECURITY_TRACK.description: Strong AV / video surveillance / access control content — typically the placement track for graduates who lean into low-voltage building systems vs. carrier / DC fiber work.
